danger_signals.visual_form: cow appearance/health degradation; chicken vulnerability to storms
danger_signals.meaning: failure to maintain care routines results in visible animal deterioration and potential death

danger_signals.visual_form: visual crop state changes when not watered
danger_signals.meaning: lack of daily water prevents crop growth; environmental stress communicated through plant visuals

danger_signals.visual_form: day/night cycle; limited business availability after dark
danger_signals.meaning: nighttime restricts player movement and access to services except the bar; environmental gating of opportunity

danger_signals.visual_form: weather event triggering chickens left outside to die
danger_signals.meaning: environmental hazards pose concrete threat to livestock; player must plan around weather

safety_signals.meaning: festivals represent safe, structured social interaction; predictable community rhythm

safety_signals.visual_form: visual environmental transformation as player clears land and develops farm
safety_signals.meaning: clearing debris and improving farm aesthetics signals player progress and successful stewardship

safety_signals.visual_form: repeated NPC interactions at specific locations (bar, town)
safety_signals.meaning: predictable NPC presence and dialogue establishes trust and social safety

safety_signals.visual_form: healthy animal appearance when properly fed, brushed, and talked to
safety_signals.meaning: visible animal wellness indicates successful care routines and stability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: bar as social hub; church as cultural landmark (with Shinto references in Japanese version, Christian cross in Western versions)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: town layout with accessible buildings; bar as evening gathering point; church representing cultural/spiritual identity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: farm environment; livestock; crops; weather patterns
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: wild dogs as predators; storms as environmental threats
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: foraging opportunities; seasonal crop cycles; day/night transitions; weather events

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: overgrown/disrepaired farm environment; degraded animal appearance; crop stagnation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: animals become sick and die; crops fail to grow; player character portrayal as failing steward

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: cleared land; improved farm aesthetics; healthy livestock appearance; growing crops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: animals thrive with proper care; crops flourish with daily watering; villager recognition of improvement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: environmental visual changes reflecting seasons; crop growth stages; weather variations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: different crops plantable in different seasons; festival calendar changes; NPC activity shifts with season

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: marriage milestone represented visually; family formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased NPC interaction frequency; marriage as core social goal completion; affection tracking (max 999, unused 1000 state)
business hours restrict player action; bar becomes only accessible location after dark; sleep cycle enforced

environmental_storytelling.example: farm begins in disrepaired state; player gradually clears land and improves visual aesthetics; progression shown through environment transformation rather than numbers
cow health visually deteriorates if neglected; requires talking to, brushing, and milking to maintain; sickness and death consequences communicate care simulation stakes
villagers gather at bar after dark; church presence establishes cultural identity (localized between Shinto and Christian versions); locations signal community values
visual crop state changes communicate growth progress without numeric displays; watering necessity shown through plant appearance rather than UI
storms pose visual and mechanical danger to exposed chickens; seasonal visual changes indicate crop availability; natural environment communicates constraints
marriage goal provides core social narrative objective; family formation visually represents completion of life-sim progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: appears well-maintained; produces milk when regularly talked to, brushed, and fed
becomes sick if not fed for one day; may die if sickness ignored; visual degradation communicates state
safely contained; requires only daily feeding to maintain productivity
can be blown away in storms or eaten by wild dogs if left outside; death risk forces player to manage enclosure
visual growth progression; reaches harvest readiness
visual stagnation; fails to grow despite passage of time; no death penalty but no progress
present at predictable locations; responds to player dialogue; affection system tracks relationship depth
farm visually improves; villagers acknowledge progress; marriage becomes achievable goal
farm degrades visually; animals sicken/die; crops fail; progression halts

notes: Harvest Moon communicates world-state primarily through environmental visuals rather than numeric UI, reflecting design principle to 'reduce digits on screen to help with immersion.' The game uses animal health appearance, crop growth stages, and farm aesthetic degradation as primary feedback mechanisms. Danger signals are mostly environmental (storms, disease, starvation) rather than combat-based, fitting the non-confrontational game design. Faction identity is implicit rather than explicit—the village community (bar, church, NPCs) versus the natural world (livestock, crops, weather). Localization differences (Shinto vs. Christian church symbolism, alcohol vs. juice references) affect cultural world-state interpretation but not core manifestation mechanics. Time-gating (day/night cycle) and resource management (animal care, crop watering) are primary world-state controllers.
